title: Steampunk Aerial Photography Rig
description: A stork carries a steampunk camera through the sky while an antique camera on the ground tracks it. The user adjusts thrust, drag, and wind forces on the stork, managing momentum and energy conservation across coupled systems. An undo/redo timeline lets the user revert force adjustments.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap/CDN) with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls. Load AntiqueCamera.glb, steampunk_camera.glb, and stork.glb. Place the antique camera on a ground platform at y=0 as a tracking station. Attach the steampunk camera beneath the stork model using a visible spring-like tether (a line or cylinder). The stork starts hovering at y=8. Implement a simplified 2D physics simulation (XZ horizontal + Y vertical) with: (1) thrust force vector the user sets via two sliders (horizontal angle 0-360, magnitude 0-20), (2) vertical lift slider (-10 to 10), (3) a wind force vector with direction and strength sliders, (4) linear drag proportional to velocity, and (5) gravity pulling the stork down. Each simulation step conserves momentum: net force = thrust + wind + gravity - drag. The steampunk camera hanging below the stork swings as a damped pendulum reacting to the stork's acceleration. The antique camera on the ground auto-rotates to always face the stork (tracking). Display a HUD showing: kinetic energy (0.5*m*v^2), potential energy (m*g*h), total mechanical energy, velocity vector, and momentum. Total energy should be approximately conserved when thrust and wind are zero. The user must navigate the stork through 3 translucent checkpoint rings (torus geometries) placed at different positions and heights. Provide an undo/redo system: each slider change is recorded as a state snapshot. Undo (Ctrl+Z) and redo (Ctrl+Y) buttons restore previous force configurations. Use directional light plus ambient light, a gradient sky background, and soft shadows. The ground should be a textured plane. Add a reset button to return the stork to the start. When all 3 checkpoints are reached, display a completion message with total energy expended. The interaction requires at least 10 deliberate slider adjustments across the force inputs to navigate all checkpoints.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  storkPositionX: number,         // stork X coordinate in world space
  storkPositionY: number,         // stork Y coordinate (altitude) in world space
  storkPositionZ: number,         // stork Z coordinate in world space
  storkVelocityX: number,         // stork velocity component along X axis in units/sec
  storkVelocityY: number,         // stork velocity component along Y axis in units/sec
  storkVelocityZ: number,         // stork velocity component along Z axis in units/sec
  thrustAngle: number,         // horizontal thrust direction in degrees (0-360)
  thrustMagnitude: number,         // thrust force magnitude (0-20)
  verticalLift: number,         // vertical lift force (-10 to 10)
  windDirection: number,         // wind force direction in degrees (0-360)
  windStrength: number,         // wind force magnitude
  kineticEnergy: number,         // kinetic energy of stork (0.5*m*v^2)
  potentialEnergy: number,         // gravitational potential energy (m*g*h)
  totalEnergy: number,         // sum of kinetic and potential energy
  cameraSwingAngle: number,         // pendulum swing angle of hanging steampunk camera in degrees
  antiqueTrackerRotation: number,         // Y-axis rotation of ground antique camera tracking the stork in radians
  undoStackSize: number,         // number of states available in undo history
  isPhysicsActive: boolean,         // whether physics simulation is running
}
